observations.recommendation:
We recommended for Management to:
Direct the City Accountant to issue written notices/demand letters to the concerned accountable officer to require the immediate settlement thereof pursuant to COA Circular       No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 and/or impose the withholding of the salaries of those presently employed personnel and elected officials as provided for under paragraphs 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated       February 10, 1997.
Ensure strict adherence on the rules and regulations on the grant and liquidation of cash advances as prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and other applicable laws, rules and regulations, such that advances shall only be granted to permanently appointed officials and employees of the City and that liquidation of the same shall be within the prescribed period.
Require the City Accountant to act on the request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances and other receivable accounts, particularly those for deceased accountable officers, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated           December 19, 2016.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Create an Inspectorate Committee with adequate number of members to complete the conduct of physical inventory within the prescribed timeframe, who will coordinate with the City General Services Office (CGSO) in the preparation of the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) for every semester of a given year, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated       June 20, 2002. Likewise, the CGSO will coordinate with the Accounting Office for the periodic reconciliation of both records, as prescribed in the said COA Circular.
Consider increasing the number of personnel in the CGSO to address the manpower constraints in the said Office and facilitate the maintenance of the required records and reports.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the City Accounting Office to review all transactions pertaining to transfer of inventory and property, plant and equipment accounts as well as the related authorization and documentations in compliance with Section 381 of Republic Act No. 7160, Section 185 of COA Circular 92-386 dated  October 20, 1992 and Section 77 of Presidential Decree 1445 to ensure that appropriate accounts are used in recording in the books of accounts in compliance with the Revised Chart of Accounts (RCA) as prescribed in Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for transactions affecting real accounts and the restated financial statements to reflect corrections of the prior period erroneous recording of expenditures, and to provide appropriate disclosures of the restatements made in the Notes to the Financial Statements for said period of adjustments or when applicable.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Disseminate to the personnel concerned, which in this case, the City Agriculturist, the rules and regulations prescribed under Section 151 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 on the notice of loss and the application of relief when loss of government supplies or property occurs to ensure filing of the request for the relief of accountability within the prescribed period.
Require the City Agriculturist to regularly submit the report of inventory of the Breeding Stocks to the City Accountant as the latter’s basis for the recording of the increase or decrease of the said asset.
Instruct the General Services Officer to conduct market survey to determine the prevailing market value or the fair value as well as the cost to sell of the remaining breeding stocks as prescribed in IPSAS 27 and facilitate the disposal of the same in accordance with Title V of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
Instruct the City Accountant and the City Agriculturist to regularly reconcile the balances of the Biological Assets – Breeding Stocks accounts and immediately prepare valid adjustments, if any, on both of their records.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the heads of the Human Resource Office and City Health Office to ensure the correctness of the computations on the claims for Active Hazard Duty Pay (AHDP) and such other eligible benefits, in compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations.
Submit justifications on the noted excess and underpayment of AHDP, without prejudice to the refund by the concerned personnel of the excess grant of AHDP and the payment to the concerned frontline HRHs of the underpaid AHDP.

With the assistance of the Project Coordinator, we recommended that Management make representations with the DBM not to return the unobligated amount of the LGSF-COVID-19 financial assistance or request for an extension to utilize the same. Otherwise, direct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to return to the National Treasury, the unobligated funds as of December 31, 2021, in compliance with Item 3.9 of the Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 128 dated September 17, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Local Finance Committee to:

Ensure that the financial information is made available to the public, in adherence to the prescribed guidelines; and
Send a written notice to the Secretary of DBM, Secretary of DILG, Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines, and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance upon posting. Also, submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the said written notice.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the CDRRM Council, in coordination with the City Accounting Office, to prepare the LDRRMF Investment Plan in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
Ensure proper coordination among the CDRRM Office and the Accounting Office on prior year’s unexpended/unobligated balances of the LDRRMF, changes in the planned program, projects and activities relating to the fund as well as in the preparation of the LDRRMF Utilization Report.

We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accountant to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents, as enumerated in Annex A, in accordance with Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
Moreover, the City Accountant together with the City Treasurer to revisit the system/procedures in the submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents between their Offices up to the submission to the COA to determine the gaps in the controls and to install applicable controls to ensure the timely and complete submission of the same to COA.

observations.recommendation: 1.11	We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and expenses not related to COVID-19 instead, spend the amounts to PPAs that can help strengthen the prevention and mitigation measures; and to adhere strictly to the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 in the utilization of the BGCM Fund of the City.

2.8  	We recommended that Management:
Comply with the given procedures in the checking of eligibility requirements of the prospective bidders and observe adherence to the provisions of the abovementioned Circular and IRR;
Require the BAC Secretariat to forward to the GSO the BAC Resolution recommending Award to the winning bidder and certification that current and updated eligibility documents of the winning bidders are on file; and
Require the City Accountant to check the completeness of documentary requirements before the submission of DVs for approval for payment by the City Mayor.

3.9	We recommended that Management stop the charging of expenses not related to the purpose of the DRRM Fund, taking into consideration the existing rules and regulations governing the utilization of the said fund.

5.14	We recommended that Management provide breakdown or specify the PPAs for COVID-19 in the LDRRMFIP to comply with the provisions on budgeting as required in Sections 321 and 336 of RA 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 124 dated March 26, 2020.

6.7	We recommended that Management:
Require the City Treasurer to endorse to the City Accountant the unclaimed checks with its original DV and supporting documents, and the latter to record the transactions as Accounts Payable; and
Require the City Treasurer to monitor the status of checks issued   by sending notices to the payee at least one month before the check becomes stale and to follow instruction on handling stale checks.

7.11  	We recommended that Management hasten the follow up on the liquidation of the SDO and compel the same to settle the balances and immediately record in the books of accounts, the submitted Reports of Disbursement from the SDO, if there are any; and to reconcile the Treasurer’s cash books with Accounting records.

8.9	We recommended that the City Accounting Office exert extra effort to reconcile the reciprocal accounts in all funds maintained by the City by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers; and to effect immediate, proper and verifiable adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct account balances, thus ensuring the presentation of reliable data on the financial reports of the City. Likewise, a periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made to ensure the accuracy of the reciprocal accounts.

9.11		We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE and Inventory, and to prepare the required reports thereon and submit the same to the Accounting Office so that reconciliation on the results of the count and accounting records can be attained and that the reliability of the balances of said accounts can be ascertained.

10.7	We recommended that Management require the City Agriculturist to submit the report of inventory of the Breeding Stocks to the City Accountant as the latter’s basis for the recording of the increase or decrease of the said asset, likewise, reconcile   the balances of the Biological Assets – Breeding Stocks with inventory and prepare valid adjustments.

11.12 	We recommended that Management require the City Accountant and the General Services Officer to periodically reconcile their records and adjust immediately the differences noted and the GSO to regularly submit the Report of Fuel Consumption to the Accounting Office for immediate recording of the same to the books of accounts of the City to prevent recurrence of the discrepancy.

12.6	We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accountant to generate bank statements online or secure snapshots from the bank;
Require the Budget Officer to prepare and submit the SAAOB monthly or quarterly; and
Upgrade the Accounting and Budget Offices by requesting COA to install or provide eNGAS (electronic New Government Accounting System) and eBudget System.

13.9	We recommended that Management require the City Accountant and the Treasurer to agree on procedures that can resolve the issue regarding the submission of DVs and its supporting documents; and Submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents in accordance with Section 100 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

14.12	We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accountant to check and verify the completeness of documents attached to the claims before forwarding the same for approval by the Chief Executive; and
Require the City Accountant to ensure compliance to DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated     November 4, 2020; and likewise, the City Engineer be required to submit the necessary documents of completed projects for COA technical inspection.

15.9	We recommended Management to enforce strict compliance on the provision of the IRR of RA 9184 by finding ways in reducing the number of days delay in the preparation and signing of the contracts of agreement to avoid the delay in the implementation of priority projects.

16.16 	We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accountant to check and verify the completeness of documents attached to the claims before forwarding the same for approval by the Chief Executive; and
Instruct the General Services Officer to request for complete documents as basis for purchase requests such as menus to be included in the budgetary requirements for sports and other activities.  Further, require them to submit an exit report every after activity.

We recommend that the Special Disbursing Officer:

Regularly update her cashbook on cash advances, foot and total monthly all entries thereon so that cash balances at a given period can easily be determined;
Prepare Liquidation Reports and return salaries, wages, honoraria and other benefits to the cashier before another cash advance is drawn to avoid accumulation of unliquidated cash advances; and
Submit to the Accounting Office, disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports within the period prescribed; and
Be assigned additional manpower and regularly monitored by the City Treasurer in as far as submission of reports and updating of her cashbook is concerned to ensure correctness of her accountabilities.

Provide for separate appropriations or increase the appropriations in the ensuing year, for the maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay of the CDRRM Office to support its operations.
18.7	Management’s comments, as contained in its letter-reply dated March 15, 2018, is quoted below:
On Traveling Expenses–These expenses were incurred during responder’s travel to different medical facilities in transporting patients with emergency cases. Having been informed through this observation, we apologize for such inappropriate charging; nevertheless we will refrain from charging these expenditures to CDRRMF.
On Internet Expenses – These expenses were utilized for the internet connectivity of the Command Center, the central data repository of the CDRRMO. Internet connection was used for Early Warning. Two ultrasonic water level gauges directly transfer real time water level readings to the command center using the internet. Local Weather Station also uses the internet to monitor weather disturbances. CCTV cameras are also connected to the command center through internet connectivity.
On Mobile Telephone Expenses – These mobile expenses were utilized in the daily operation’s communication system of the Command Center, for payment of the center’s hotline numbers.
On Office Furniture – It was presumed that source of fund is Gawad Kalasag Award – Trust Fund (from Office of the Civil Defense Caraga).
On Office Supplies and Other Supplies and Materials – These office supplies were purchased for disaster preparedness training purposes. However, all Purchase Requests with supplies items were unfortunately put together by the BAC Secretariat and were “lump” during the bidding. Thus, these training supplies were included in other regular office supplies in the JEV issued by the Accounting Office.
On Fuel – These expenditures were used for the DRR operations of the CDRRMO during occurrence of an incident or a disaster. However, this also includes transporting patients to different hospitals were not included in RA 10121 directives. We regret for charging those expenditures not illegible to DRRM fund like referral services. At present, we adhere with the mandate and refrain from charging not related expenses to CDRRMF.
On Repair and Maintenance – These expenses were used for the repair and maintenance of DRRM vehicles.
On Cooking Equipment – Cooking Equipment was purchased purposely for Operation Center activation’s use. This expenditure also includes the purchase of LED monitor for weather disturbance monitoring purposes. It is installed in the DRRM Operation Center.
On IT Supplies and Accessories – These were purchased for DRR training purposes. Some items were for repair and replacement of the damaged computer accessories of the Command Center.
18.8 	The Audit Team acknowledged the commitment of the Management to adhere with the applicable guidelines on the charging of expenditures to CDRRMF but rejoined that the above mentioned expenses should be charged against the General Fund of the City.
